---
name: Feature request
about: Suggest a new capability, parity improvement, or ergonomic Rust API addition.
title: ""
labels: enhancement
assignees: techouse
---

<!--
    Before filing a feature request, please check:

    - upstream qs issues: https://github.com/ljharb/qs/issues
    - Rust divergence policy:
      https://github.com/techouse/qs_rust/blob/master/docs/divergences.md

    qs_rust intentionally does not mirror every JS/Foundation/JVM/.NET runtime
    behavior, so requests are easier to evaluate when they explain why the feature
    belongs in the Rust crate specifically.
-->

## Problem Statement

<!--
What problem are you trying to solve? If this is parity-related, describe the missing
behavior or API precisely.
-->

## Proposed Solution

<!--
Describe the API or behavior you want. Rust-specific examples are especially helpful.
-->

```rust
// Optional example of the desired API
```

## Motivation And Use Case

<!--
Why should this exist in qs_rust?
- parity with Node qs
- parity with sibling ports
- Rust ergonomics
- integration with serde/chrono/time
- performance or memory behavior
-->

## Compatibility / Scope

- [ ] This should match upstream Node `qs`
- [ ] This should follow sibling-port behavior even if Node differs
- [ ] This is a Rust-specific ergonomic addition

If applicable, link the relevant references:

- Upstream Node `qs` issue or docs:
- Sibling-port implementation or issue:
- Existing Rust divergence entry:

## Alternatives Considered

<!--
Describe alternatives, workarounds, or why the current hooks/options are not enough.
-->

## Possible Downsides

<!--
List any tradeoffs you see: API complexity, performance cost, semver risk, additional
feature flags, etc.
-->

## Additional Context

<!--
Add code samples, benchmarks, screenshots, or related discussion links here.
-->
